title: You Know What I Mean
content:
혻혻혻혻I占퐒e noticed this happen, when everything is black,
혻혻혻혻When I占퐉 down below zero and cannot get back,
혻혻혻혻When I feel like a sort of a National Debt,
혻혻혻혻That will go on for ages and never be met,
혻혻혻혻When my will is all bagged at the knees and dead beat,
혻혻혻혻It is then, don占퐐 you know, that., I占퐉 certain to meet
혻혻혻혻With some prodigal lifeless dejected old bean,
혻혻혻혻Who is worse off than I you know what I mean.

혻혻혻혻Someone or other who占퐏 entered the race,
혻혻혻혻With a sense of intention but can占퐐 stay the pace,
혻혻혻혻He tells all his troubles and heaven knows what,
혻혻혻혻Talks about Fate and all that sort of rot,
혻혻혻혻And it makes all my own little troubles look small,
혻혻혻혻Till I find I占퐒e no cause to be worried at all,
혻혻혻혻And it doesn占퐐 seem cricket to grouse when I占퐒e seen,
혻혻혻혻That he占퐏 worse off than I you know what I mean.

혻혻혻혻No matter how hard one may fall down the hill,
혻혻혻혻There占퐏 always a somebody lower down still,
혻혻혻혻And it makes you feel well, it seems mean to repeat,
혻혻혻혻All your own little troubles to people you meet.
혻혻혻혻One learns in the end, that self pity占퐏 a curse,
혻혻혻혻And to talk of your cares only makes them seem worse.
혻혻혻혻It takes courage to stand where it占퐏 easy to lean,
혻혻혻혻But it makes you feel better you know what I mean.

혻혻혻혻The chap we all like is the chap who can smile,
혻혻혻혻Though his heart may be breaking with sorrow the while,
혻혻혻혻He just keeps them all secretly locked in his breast,
혻혻혻혻Keep占퐏 the worm to himself, gives the world of his best.
혻혻혻혻He has losses like we have, yet never gives in,
혻혻혻혻But goes silently back to his task with a grin.
혻혻혻혻And the lesson we learn from this priceless old being,
혻혻혻혻Is to smile all the while with some laughs in between.
혻혻혻혻Though you占퐎e empty and broke, meet your fate with a joke,
혻혻혻혻For the sake of the folk who can占퐐 see what you mean
혻혻혻혻And it may be in turn even they will yet learn,
혻혻혻혻And they占퐇l smile all the while when they see what you mean.
혻혻혻혻Do you get me? Ah well that占퐏 what I mean.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Milton Hayes represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of John Milton Hayes's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
